properties.title: Orbán vows to keep fighting EU on first Brussels visit since election defeat
properties.description: BRUSSELS — Former Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán was in a defiant mood on his return to Brussels on Wednesday, insisting that he’s staying in politics and will continue to fight the EU on issues such as migration. Speaking to a small group of reporters overlooking the European Commission’s Berlaymont headquarters, Orbán said his “goal has not changed” when it comes to the EU. “We have to reform Brussels … It’s very difficult to find a way which provides success for Hungary if the European Union is not on the same track.” Orbán lost April’s election , but he remains the leader of the Fidesz party. properties.title: Far-right general upends Italian politics
properties.description: ROME — Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni spent years pushing Italy’s post-fascist right toward the political mainstream. Now a former army general is pulling it back. The newly launched National Future party, led by firebrand ex-paratrooper General Roberto Vannacci , is rapidly gathering support and attracting defectors from the governing coalition. This is causing a splitting political headache for Meloni, as she heads toward a general election next year looking unusually vulnerable . The first effect of Vannacci’s rise is that he is crushing one of Meloni’s key coalition allies : the anti-immigration League party of Deputy Prime Minister Matteo Salvini. properties.title: Tory veterans warn Labour: Don’t repeat our leadership chaos
properties.description: LONDON — Labour MPs want to swap out the prime minister mid-term without hurling their party — and the nation — into turmoil. There’s a recent precedent, and it’s not good. The Conservatives suffered a punishing defeat at the 2024 election after flipping through five prime ministers in seven years, including three during 2022 alone. Each leadership race tore wounds in the party that never healed and thrust MPs deeper into bitter factionalism, making stable government near-impossible. Voters will not forgive Labour unleashing a similar free-for-all, senior figures around current Prime Minister Keir Starmer have warned . properties.description: Listen on Spotify Apple Music Amazon Music Nach fast vier Monaten Blockade der Straße von Hormus gibt es Bewegung am Persischen Golf: Donald Trump drängt auf ein Abkommen mit dem Iran. Doch was wie ein diplomatischer Durchbruch klingt, könnte das Regime in Teheran sogar stärken. Im POLITICO Berlin Playbook Security Update spricht Rixa Fürsen mit Aras Nathan-Keul über die geplante Öffnung der Straße von Hormus, die globale Ölkrise, eingefrorene Milliarden für den Iran und die Frage, wer von diesem Deal wirklich profitieren würde. Es geht um das Atomprogramm, die Revolutionsgarden, Irans Proxies, die Hisbollah im Libanon, Israels Sicherheitsinteressen – und um die Menschen im Iran, die nach Krieg, Repression und wirtschaftlicher Not erneut zwischen Machtpolitik und Hoffnungslosigkeit stehen.

properties.description: Hungarian lawmakers on Monday passed a constitutional amendment that would ban Viktor Orbán from returning to power. The amendment, approved by 135 votes in favor and 50 against, would limit prime ministers to just eight years in office if it becomes law. The amendment is written to apply retroactively, meaning that Viktor Orbán could not return as Hungary’s prime minister. Orbán served as prime minister for a total of 20 years. “The restoration of the rule of law will not be born from a single law, but every true democratic reconstruction has symbolic and constitutional pillars.
